items.briefing:
Australia's own cyber security agency (ASD) has issued a warning about a rising phishing technique called "device code phishing," where attackers trick you into approving fake login requests — often disguised as legitimate IT prompts. This is being actively exploited using off-the-shelf criminal toolkits, making it easier for less sophisticated attackers to target Australian organisations including RTOs. Separately, a serious Linux security flaw has been identified that could allow attackers to gain full system control, relevant to any staff or IT teams running Linux-based systems.

Deepfake incidents are also causing real harm in school communities, a reminder that AI-generated fake images and videos are no longer just a corporate risk. **Practical tip:** If you receive an unexpected login approval request — on any device — do not accept it unless you personally just triggered a sign-in. When in doubt, contact your IT team first.

featured.title: ASD warns of device code phishing as Proofpoint tracks growing criminal toolkits
featured.quote: ASD warning on device code phishing targets millions of Australian Microsoft 365 users directly.
